prompt_verb_analysis.literal_prompt: Make me a web based Daily dashboard for all mlb teams and statistics focusing on weekly trends, upcoming games and schedules and overall rankings. Use github pages for hosting.

candidate_verbs.rejected_because: Too generic; loses the compound focus areas explicitly named in the prompt.
Captures the surface (dashboard) but drops the three substantive content blocks the prompt focuses on.

candidate_verbs.verb: show a daily dashboard of MLB teams covering weekly trends, upcoming games, and overall rankings
candidate_verbs.rationale: This is the prompt's compound deliverable verb. The user did not ask for a generic dashboard — they explicitly named the three focus areas (weekly trends, upcoming games and schedules, overall rankings) and the population (all MLB teams). Dropping any of those three or generalizing 'MLB teams' would lose load-bearing intent. This verb is what first-contact will actually be evaluated against.

prompt_verb_analysis.chosen_verb: show a daily dashboard of MLB teams covering weekly trends, upcoming games, and overall rankings

prompt_named_verb_assertion.verb_from_prompt: show a daily dashboard of MLB teams covering weekly trends, upcoming games, and overall rankings
prompt_named_verb_assertion.scenario: Open output/final/index.html in a real browser (Playwright headless Chromium loading via http://); wait for data to load (≤15s); inspect the DOM
prompt_named_verb_assertion.expected_result: The page contains (a) a rankings/standings section listing all 30 MLB teams with W-L records, (b) a weekly-trends section with last-7-days performance for each team, (c) an upcoming-games section listing games in the next 7 days, AND no error banner is shown

sections.name: data-client
sections.charter: Owns every outbound HTTP request to the MLB Stats API and is the single source of truth for the project's API surface. Exposes typed JavaScript functions that return plain data objects for teams, standings, schedule, and per-team game results. No DOM, no rendering, no business-logic computation beyond minimal response normalization. Contains the only references to fetch() and to statsapi.mlb.com URLs in the entire codebase.
sections.acceptance: Module exposes fetchTeams(), fetchStandings(season), fetchSchedule(startDate, endDate), and fetchTeamGameResults(teamId, startDate, endDate) functions. Each function performs a real HTTP GET against statsapi.mlb.com, parses the JSON response, and returns the normalized shape declared in the corresponding interface contract. Network failures and non-200 responses are surfaced as thrown errors with a recognizable error class so callers can render an error state.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: exposes fetchTeams(), fetchStandings(season), fetchSchedule(startDate, endDate), and fetchTeamGameResults(teamId, startDate, endDate)
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Static import of the data-client module from a Node test harness
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: All four named exports exist and are functions

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: performs a real HTTP GET against statsapi.mlb.com
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Call fetchTeams() from a browser running the integrated artifact via http://
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: A network request to https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/teams?sportId=1 is observed and resolves with a JSON body

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: returns the normalized shape declared in the corresponding interface contract
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Call fetchStandings(2026) and inspect the returned array
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Returned value is an array of length 30 where each element has keys teamId, league, division, wins, losses, pct, gb

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Contains the only references to fetch() and to statsapi.mlb.com URLs in the entire codebase
acceptance_assertions.scenario: grep the rest of the codebase (every JS file except data-client) for 'statsapi.mlb.com' or 'fetch('
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches outside the data-client module

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Network failures and non-200 responses are surfaced as thrown errors
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Invoke a data-client function with a deliberately malformed query and observe the rejection
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: The returned promise rejects with a named error (DataClientError or subclass)

out_of_scope: Computing trends, rankings, or any derived statistics
Caching beyond the lifetime of a single page load
Authentication (the MLB Stats API requires none)

sections.name: trends-engine
sections.charter: Pure computation module that takes raw per-team game-result arrays produced by data-client and returns trend objects per team. Computes last-7-days wins, last-7-days losses, run differential over that window, and current streak (consecutive Ws or Ls). No HTTP, no DOM. Deterministic: same input always produces same output. Handles teams with zero games in the window cleanly.
sections.acceptance: Module exposes computeWeeklyTrends(perTeamResultMap) which takes a Map of teamId -> Array of game-result objects (as defined in data-client--trends-engine contract) and returns an array of trend objects (per the trends-engine--ui-render contract). A team with no games in the window appears in the output with last7W=0, last7L=0, runDiff7=0, streak=null.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: exposes computeWeeklyTrends(perTeamResultMap)
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Static import of the trends-engine module
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Named export computeWeeklyTrends exists and is a function

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: returns an array of trend objects (per the trends-engine--ui-render contract)
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Pass a fixture map with 30 teams and a mix of W/L results over the last 7 days
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Returned array has length 30; every element has teamId, last7W (number), last7L (number), runDiff7 (number), streak (string or null)

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: A team with no games in the window appears in the output with last7W=0, last7L=0, runDiff7=0, streak=null
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Pass a map containing one team whose game-result array is empty
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Output for that team is {teamId, last7W: 0, last7L: 0, runDiff7: 0, streak: null}

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Deterministic: same input always produces same output
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Call computeWeeklyTrends with the same input twice and deep-equal the results
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Both calls return structurally equal output

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: No HTTP, no DOM
acceptance_assertions.scenario: grep the trends-engine source for 'fetch(' or 'document.' or 'window.'
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches

sections.name: rankings-engine
sections.charter: Pure computation module that takes the standings array produced by data-client and projects three views: division standings (six divisions), league standings (AL + NL), and wild-card race (top 3 non-division-leaders per league by win pct). No HTTP, no DOM. Deterministic. Preserves the canonical MLB division ordering (East, Central, West).
sections.acceptance: Module exposes computeRankings(standings) which takes the standings array (per data-client--rankings-engine contract) and returns an object {divisionStandings, leagueStandings, wildCard} matching the rankings-engine--ui-render contract. divisionStandings has exactly 6 division keys (AL_East, AL_Central, AL_West, NL_East, NL_Central, NL_West), each containing teams sorted by descending win pct. wildCard has exactly 2 league keys (AL, NL), each listing the top 3 non-division-leader teams by win pct.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: exposes computeRankings(standings)
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Static import of the rankings-engine module
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Named export computeRankings exists and is a function

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: divisionStandings has exactly 6 division keys
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Pass a fixture standings array and inspect the returned divisionStandings
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Object has exactly the keys AL_East, AL_Central, AL_West, NL_East, NL_Central, NL_West

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: each containing teams sorted by descending win pct
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Inspect any division array
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Array is sorted such that element[i].pct >= element[i+1].pct for all i

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: wildCard has exactly 2 league keys (AL, NL), each listing the top 3 non-division-leader teams
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Inspect wildCard.AL and wildCard.NL on returned object
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Both arrays have length 3 and contain no team that is the top-pct entry in its division

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: No HTTP, no DOM
acceptance_assertions.scenario: grep the rankings-engine source for 'fetch(' or 'document.' or 'window.'
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches

sections.name: ui-shell
sections.charter: Defines the static HTML scaffolding, CSS, and visual identity for the dashboard. Provides the page header with a last-updated timestamp slot, and four named container regions: standings panel, weekly-trends panel, upcoming-games panel, and an error/empty-state banner. Defines team identity styling using CSS custom properties for per-team primary colors (no team logos). Establishes the loading-state and empty-state visual designs that ui-render targets. No JavaScript logic, no data fetching.
sections.acceptance: Repository contains index.html with id'd containers for rankings (#rankings-panel), trends (#trends-panel), upcoming games (#upcoming-panel), a last-updated label (#last-updated), and an error banner (#error-banner). A CSS file defines visual hierarchy for these regions and exposes per-team color variables. The HTML loads ui-render as an ES module via <script type="module">. No external CDN runtime dependencies are referenced.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: id'd containers for rankings, trends, upcoming games, a last-updated label, and an error banner
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Load output/final/index.html and query for required element IDs
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Elements with IDs rankings-panel, trends-panel, upcoming-panel, last-updated, and error-banner all exist in the DOM

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: loads ui-render as an ES module via <script type="module">
acceptance_assertions.scenario: grep index.html for the script tag
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: A <script tag with type="module" referencing ui-render (or main.js that imports ui-render) is present

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: No external CDN runtime dependencies are referenced
acceptance_assertions.scenario: grep index.html and any CSS files for 'cdn.', 'unpkg.', 'jsdelivr', 'googleapis.com', 'cdnjs'
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: exposes per-team color variables
acceptance_assertions.scenario: grep the CSS file(s) for CSS custom properties named per team
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: At least 30 CSS custom properties matching --team-* (or equivalent) are defined

out_of_scope: Any data fetching or business logic
Bundling or referencing official MLB team logos
User-account UI, login forms, preferences panel

sections.name: ui-render
sections.charter: Glue module that orchestrates page bootstrap: calls data-client, hands raw data to trends-engine and rankings-engine, and writes the resulting view-models into the ui-shell DOM containers. Owns the loading state (initial), the error state (data-client failure), and the empty state (no games today). Updates the last-updated timestamp. Imports from data-client, trends-engine, and rankings-engine only; touches the DOM only inside named render functions.
sections.acceptance: Module exposes an init() entry point that runs on DOMContentLoaded. On success, the standings panel shows division tables with all 30 teams, the trends panel shows per-team last-7-days summaries, and the upcoming panel shows games in the next 7 days. On data-client failure, the error banner becomes visible with a human-readable message and the panels are not left in the loading state. On a no-games-today situation, the upcoming panel renders an empty-state message rather than disappearing.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: exposes an init() entry point that runs on DOMContentLoaded
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Static import of ui-render and inspect named exports; also grep for DOMContentLoaded listener
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: init export exists; the listener is registered somewhere in the module graph

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: the standings panel shows division tables with all 30 teams
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Load the integrated artifact in a browser; wait for fetch to resolve; query #rankings-panel for team rows
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: At least 30 distinct team identifiers (abbreviations) are present within #rankings-panel

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: the trends panel shows per-team last-7-days summaries
acceptance_assertions.scenario: After load, inspect #trends-panel for per-team rows
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: 30 team rows are rendered, each showing a last-7-days W-L pair and a run differential value

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: the upcoming panel shows games in the next 7 days
acceptance_assertions.scenario: After load, inspect #upcoming-panel for game entries
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: At least one game entry is rendered with a date within the next 7 days, OR (on a true off-day window, statistically rare) the empty-state copy is visible

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: On data-client failure, the error banner becomes visible
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Stub data-client to throw; load page
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: #error-banner has visible:true (not display:none) and contains a non-empty human-readable message

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: On a no-games-today situation, the upcoming panel renders an empty-state message
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Stub data-client to return an empty schedule; load page
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: #upcoming-panel contains visible copy indicating 'no games scheduled' (or equivalent empty-state text)

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: Updates the last-updated timestamp
acceptance_assertions.scenario: After successful load, read #last-updated text
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Non-empty text containing a parseable timestamp from the current day

out_of_scope: Computing trends or rankings directly (delegated to engines)
Performing fetch calls directly (delegated to data-client)

sections.name: edge-case-testing
sections.charter: Cover every acceptance assertion with verifier: edge_case_testing from the section list, exercised under production-fidelity environment — no substitution of runtime dependencies. Loads the integrated artifact through a real HTTP server (not file://), drives a headless real browser (Playwright Chromium), and performs the live fetch against statsapi.mlb.com unless an explicit fixture-replay mode is invoked. Also exercises the empty-state path (stubbed empty schedule) and the error-banner path (stubbed thrown error), then restores live mode. Confirms every FC and the PNV.1 assertion.
sections.acceptance: Test suite runs in CI-style fashion from a Node harness, serves the output/final/ directory via a local http server, launches Playwright Chromium headless, and exercises three scenarios: (1) live load against statsapi.mlb.com, (2) empty-schedule stub, (3) data-client-throw stub. All edge_case_testing assertions from S1-S5 plus the PNV.1 assertion are evaluated and reported pass/fail. The suite exits non-zero if any assertion fails.

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: serves the output/final/ directory via a local http server
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Inspect the test runner script
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Runner starts an http server (e.g., http-server, node:http) on a localhost port and points Playwright at it; no file:// URLs used

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: exercises three scenarios
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Inspect test files
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Three distinct test cases exist: live-load, empty-schedule-stub, data-client-throw-stub

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: All edge_case_testing assertions from S1-S5 plus the PNV.1 assertion are evaluated
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Run the suite and inspect the report
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Every assertion id with verifier=edge_case_testing or verifier=prompt_named_verb from sections-v1.json appears in the report with a pass/fail verdict

acceptance_assertions.from_acceptance_phrase: exits non-zero if any assertion fails
acceptance_assertions.scenario: Force one assertion to fail and run the suite
acceptance_assertions.expected_result: Process exit code is non-zero

inflection_resolutions.topic: Data source for MLB schedule, standings, and team statistics
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Canonical, free, no-API-key, CORS-permissive JSON feed maintained by MLB Advanced Media. Documented broadly across community projects (toddrob99/MLB-StatsAPI, dozens of JS clients on GitHub). Only viable choice for a static GitHub-Pages dashboard that fetches client-side.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: data-client module sources all data from https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: All fetch URLs in data-client begin with https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: No third-party scraper or aggregator endpoint is referenced
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: Zero references to espn.com, sports-data-api, or similar aggregators

inflection_resolutions.topic: How 'daily' freshness is delivered on a static host
inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Client-side fetch on every page load
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Simplest path that genuinely delivers each visitor today's data. Requires zero CI infrastructure. The CORS sub-question is resolved via canonical evidence below (statsapi.mlb.com returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *).

cors_verification.verbatim_excerpt_note: statsapi.mlb.com endpoints respond with the header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' on all public /api/v1/ paths. This is widely documented in community projects that consume the API from browser clients (e.g., https://github.com/brianhaferkamp/mlbdata, plus many static-page MLB-stats demos hosted on github.io). The probe is recorded as research/probes/probe-cors/ for traceability; empirical re-confirmation occurs during edge-case-testing's live-load scenario.
cors_verification.fallback_if_wrong: If runtime CORS fails (caught by edge-case-testing's live-load scenario), fallback is GitHub Actions cron building a JSON snapshot — would re-enter Editor with a section amendment introducing snapshot-builder. Not selected in v1 because evidence is strong.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Page calls data-client functions on load; no CI-built snapshot file is read
No pre-baked snapshot JSON file is committed under output/final/
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: No file like standings-snapshot.json or schedule-snapshot.json present

inflection_resolutions.rationale: Prompt frames the dashboard around teams (all mlb teams, overall rankings). Player drill-down explicitly in Discovery out_of_scope. Team-level is the minimum-viable load-bearing reading of the prompt.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: No player-leaderboard or player-roster rendering exists

inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Rolling last-7-days W-L + run differential delta per team
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Maps literally to 'weekly'; more meaningful for a daily dashboard than calendar-week (which would be empty on a Monday morning).

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: trends-engine queries data over a 7-day rolling window ending today
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: Window passed to data-client is [today-6, today] inclusive (or equivalent 7-day inclusive range)

inflection_resolutions.rationale: Matches the 'daily' framing while giving enough lookahead. Mirrors the 7-day window already chosen for trends.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: ui-render requests schedule for today through today+7
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: fetchSchedule is called with startDate=today, endDate=today+7

inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Six division standings tables + wild-card race
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Canonical MLB ranking presentation. Wild-card race adds postseason-positioning value at low cost.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: rankings-engine output has 6 divisionStandings keys and 2 wildCard keys
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: {divisionStandings: {AL_East, AL_Central, AL_West, NL_East, NL_Central, NL_West}, wildCard: {AL, NL}}

inflection_resolutions.topic: Behavior on off-days or when API returns no games
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Preserves layout; honest. Substituting yesterday's data would mislead a user reading a 'today' dashboard.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: When schedule is empty, #upcoming-panel shows empty-state text
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: Visible copy contains 'no games' or 'No games scheduled' (case-insensitive)

inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Team abbreviations + official primary color swatches
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Avoids logo licensing/hotlinking risk; abbreviations are unambiguous for a baseball audience; color swatches are visually meaningful.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: No image files for team logos are bundled and no MLB CDN logos are hotlinked
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: No .png/.svg files named after teams; no img src referencing mlbstatic.com or similar logo CDNs

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: Per-team CSS color custom properties exist
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: At least 30 CSS custom properties matching --team-* are defined

inflection_resolutions.td_introduction_rationale: Discovery flagged weekly trends as a content focus but did not specify a chart library. This is a TD-introduced inflection because library choice forks the build (zero-dep vs CDN dep vs bundler), affects production-fidelity (CDN deps are network-dependent), and is not load-bearing on Discovery's stated intent.
choices: No chart library; inline SVG sparklines built by hand
inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: No chart library; inline SVG sparklines built by hand
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Zero external dependencies; satisfies the production-fidelity guidance (no CDN runtime deps). A 7-point sparkline is trivially expressible as a <polyline> in SVG. Avoids the Chart.js footprint for what is essentially a 7-point per-team line.

machine_checkable_assertions.statement: No Chart.js or other charting library is loaded
machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: Zero references to chart.js, chartjs, d3, echarts, plotly, highcharts
Rendered trends panel contains <svg> elements with <polyline> or <path> per team

inflection_resolutions.td_introduction_rationale: Pure technical choice the user didn't specify; forks code organization across the entire build (modules vs IIFE vs bundler).
inflection_resolutions.rationale: GitHub Pages serves ES modules with correct MIME natively. No bundler in the build pipeline. Easier section isolation (each section is literally one .js file). Modern browser support is universal.

machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: A <script tag with type="module" attribute is present
No webpack/rollup/esbuild generated bundle files; .js files are hand-authored ES modules

inflection_resolutions.td_introduction_rationale: User didn't specify a CSS framework; this is a TD choice that affects bundle size and external deps.
choices: Vanilla CSS with CSS custom properties
inflection_resolutions.chosen_branch: Vanilla CSS with CSS custom properties
inflection_resolutions.rationale: Zero external dependencies. CSS custom properties handle the per-team color theming cleanly. Dashboard layout is straightforward grid; doesn't justify Tailwind's footprint.

machine_checkable_assertions.expected_value: Zero references to tailwind, bootstrap, bulma, foundation

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Build a static web page that displays a daily-updated snapshot of all 30 MLB teams: overall standings/rankings, weekly performance trends (last-7-days W-L and run differential), and upcoming game schedules.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Load output/final/index.html via http://localhost and inspect DOM
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All three content blocks (rankings, weekly trends, upcoming schedule) are present and all 30 teams appear somewhere in the DOM

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: The deliverable is a single web page loaded in a desktop browser by visiting a URL.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Inspect output/final/ for a single entry index.html
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Exactly one HTML entry file exists; no multi-page routing

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Dashboard covers all 30 current MLB teams.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: After load, count unique team abbreviations in the DOM
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: At least 30 unique MLB team abbreviations are present

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Three primary content blocks: weekly trends, upcoming games, overall rankings.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Query DOM for the three named panel containers and verify each has rendered content
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: #rankings-panel, #trends-panel, and #upcoming-panel all contain non-empty rendered children

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Stats are team-level rather than player-level.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: grep codebase for player-stat or roster fetch patterns
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No fetches against /people/, /roster/, or player-leaderboard endpoints

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: 'Daily' is achieved via client-side fetch on page load.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Load page in browser and observe network requests
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Network requests to statsapi.mlb.com fire on page load

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Weekly trends = each team's last-7-days W-L + run differential.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Inspect a rendered trend row for fixture team
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Row exposes last-7-days W-L pair and a numeric run differential

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Upcoming games = today + next 7 days.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All listed game dates fall within [today, today+7]

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Overall rankings = division standings.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Six division-named tables/sections (AL East, AL Central, AL West, NL East, NL Central, NL West) are present

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Read-only, no auth, no user accounts.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No login UI, no auth client, no localStorage write of user preferences

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Build is on Windows host; deliverable is browser-agnostic.
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Inspect build scripts and section file paths
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No POSIX-only shell invocations in any build script; .js modules use forward-slash imports (universal)

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: 2026 season is in progress mid-May; live data expected.
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: statsapi.mlb.com returns non-empty standings for season=2026 and non-empty schedule for today

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Gambling, betting odds, or sportsbook integration

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Fantasy baseball
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches for 'fantasy', 'draft', 'lineup-set'

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Ticket purchasing
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches for 'ticket', 'stubhub', 'seatgeek', 'vivid'

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Push notifications, email digests
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches for 'Notification(', 'serviceWorker', 'push', 'email', 'sendgrid'

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: User accounts, authentication, saved preferences
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches for 'login', 'signup', 'OAuth', 'firebase.auth', 'localStorage.setItem'

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Player-level statistics
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero references to /people/, /roster/, player leaderboards

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Server-side backend, database, non-static infrastructure
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No server runtime files (no Express/Fastify/serverless functions); deliverable is static assets only

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Bundled official MLB team logos or licensed imagery
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Inspect output/final for image assets and HTML img tags
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No team-logo image files bundled; no img src referencing mlbstatic.com

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Minor league, international league, non-MLB content
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All endpoint calls use sportId=1 (MLB only); no references to other sportIds or leagues

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Commentary, news articles, other sports scores
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: No fetches to news endpoints; no NFL/NBA/NHL references

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Multi-language localization
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Zero matches for 'i18n', 'intl', 'translate', 'lang switcher'

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Data source = MLB Stats API
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All fetch URLs target https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Freshness via client-side fetch on every load
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Live fetch calls observed on each load; no pre-baked snapshot file referenced

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Team-level granularity only
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Team rows only; no player rows or player drill-down

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Weekly trends = rolling 7-day window
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Window is [today-6, today] inclusive (7-day rolling)

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Upcoming = today + next 7 days
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: All dates fall within [today, today+7]

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Six division standings + wild-card race
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: {divisionStandings has 6 keys, wildCard has 2 keys}

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Empty-state copy on no-games days
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Stub data-client to return empty schedule; observe UI
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: #upcoming-panel shows visible empty-state copy

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Abbreviations + color swatches (no logos)
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Each team rendered with abbreviation text + color swatch; no tags for logos

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Build targets MLB (Major League Baseball)
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Inspect data-client fetch URLs and rendered content
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Data sourced from statsapi.mlb.com with sportId=1; rendered teams match the 30 MLB clubs

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Hosted on GitHub Pages
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Verify final deliverable URL is reachable
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: https://mondrianaire.github.io/auto-builder/runs/mlb-daily-dashboard/output/final/ returns HTTP 200 with the dashboard HTML

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Page loads successfully, no blank screen, no console-fatal errors
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Headless browser load of the live URL
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: HTTP 200; DOM body has visible content; console has no error-severity messages

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Within seconds, dashboard renders actual current MLB data
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: After load, wait ≤15s; inspect panels for content
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Rankings, trends, and upcoming panels contain real team data (not 'Loading...', not error)

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: All three focus areas visible: weekly trends, upcoming games, overall rankings
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Query DOM for all three panel containers
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: #trends-panel, #upcoming-panel, #rankings-panel all visible and populated

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: All 30 MLB teams represented
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Count unique team abbreviations across the DOM
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: 30 unique team abbreviations are present

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Data is recognizably current
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Inspect schedule dates and last-updated timestamp
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Schedule contains dates within the next 7 days; last-updated timestamp is within the current day

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Page is hosted at a publicly reachable URL on GitHub Pages and URL is delivered
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Verify deployment + dashboard URL is in run report
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: URL https://mondrianaire.github.io/auto-builder/runs/mlb-daily-dashboard/output/final/ is live and surfaced in the deliverable handoff

discovery_coverage_assertions.covers_text: Next-day return visit shows refreshed data
discovery_coverage_assertions.scenario: Verify ui-render always re-fetches; no stale cached data path
discovery_coverage_assertions.expected_result: Each page load triggers fresh fetches; data is not pinned to a build-time snapshot
